mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand growth that supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause immediate negative equity price adjustments.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in 55% of contraction periods or the signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 315 signals persistent inflation that forces equity investors to demand higher risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 315 or the predictive IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or produces negative returns in 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and rising retail sales indicates a shift from durable goods to services consumption that weakens capital expenditure forecasts and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 60-day returns in 50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization that boosts short-term earnings momentum and triggers positive immediate equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not occur in 55% of expansion periods within the recovery regime or the signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05).
